connection_attempt.c(3) Library Functions Manual connection_attempt.c(3)

NAME

connection_attempt.c - Connection state machine and attempt logic for TCP/WebSocket clients.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


const char * connection_state_name (connection_state_t state)
Get human-readable state name for logging. asciichat_error_t connection_context_init (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Initialize connection attempt context. void connection_context_cleanup (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Cleanup connection attempt context. asciichat_error_t connection_state_transition (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, connection_state_t new_state)
Transition to next connection state with validation. bool connection_check_timeout (const connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Check if connection attempt has exceeded timeout. asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_tcp (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, const char *server_address, uint16_t server_port)
Attempt direct TCP connection. asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_websocket (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, const char *ws_url)
Attempt WebSocket connection (ws:// or wss://).

Detailed Description

Connection state machine and attempt logic for TCP/WebSocket clients.

Implements direct TCP and WebSocket connections for client mode. Manages connection state transitions, timeouts, and transport lifecycle.

Features:

  • Direct TCP connection to server
  • WebSocket connection support
  • Timeout management
  • Crypto handshake integration
  • ACIP transport creation
  • Proper resource cleanup

Integration Points:

  • Called from src/client/main.c connection loop
  • Returns active transport when connection succeeds

Date

January 2026

Version

2.0

Definition in file connection_attempt.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_tcp (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx, const char * server_address, uint16_t server_port)

Attempt direct TCP connection. Attempt TCP connection.

Connects to server via TCP, performs crypto handshake if enabled, and creates ACIP transport for protocol communication. <Encryption only (no identity verification)

<Full crypto: encryption + authentication

<Authentication only (no payload encryption)

<No encryption, no authentication (plaintext only)

<Encryption only (no identity verification)

<Authentication only (no payload encryption)

<Encryption only (no identity verification)

<Full crypto: encryption + authentication

<Authentication only (no payload encryption)

<No encryption, no authentication (plaintext only)

<Encryption only (no identity verification)

<Authentication only (no payload encryption)

<No encryption, no authentication (plaintext only)

Definition at line 168 of file connection_attempt.c.

169                                                                {
170   log_info("=== connection_attempt_tcp CALLED: address='%s', port=%u ===", server_address, server_port);
171 
172   if (!ctx || !server_address) {
173     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
174   }
175 
176   // Check if shutdown was requested before attempting connection (via callback if available)
177   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
178     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection attempt aborted due to shutdown request");
179   }
180 
181   // Normalize the endpoint once so TCP and WebSocket paths use the same rules.
182   connection_endpoint_t endpoint = {0};
183   asciichat_error_t endpoint_result = connection_endpoint_resolve(server_address, server_port, &endpoint);
184   if (endpoint_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
185     return endpoint_result;
186   }
187 
188   log_debug("connection_attempt_tcp: server_address='%s', normalized=%s host=%s port=%u", server_address,
189             connection_endpoint_protocol_name(endpoint.protocol), endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
190 
191   if (endpoint.protocol == CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET) {
192     // WebSocket connection path
193     const char *ws_url = endpoint.input;
194 
195     // Set server IP/port for crypto handshake known_hosts verification
196     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(server_connection_set_ip, endpoint.host);
197     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_INT(server_connection_set_port, endpoint.port);
198     log_debug("Set server IP=%s, port=%u for WebSocket crypto handshake", endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
199 
200     log_info("Attempting WebSocket connection to %s", ws_url);
201 
202     // Transition to attempting state
203     asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
204     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
205       return result;
206     }
207 
208     // Set timeout for this attempt
209     ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
210     ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP; // Use same timeout as TCP
211 
212     // Compute crypto mode from CLI options
213     // Determine if client has authentication material (keys or password)
214     bool has_auth_material =
215         (GET_OPTION(encrypt_key)[0] != '\0' || GET_OPTION(num_identity_keys) > 0 || GET_OPTION(password)[0] != '\0');
216     bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
217     bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
218 
219     uint8_t crypto_mode;
220     if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && !has_auth_material)
221       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_ENCRYPT; // default: encrypt only
222     else if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
223       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_FULL; // full: encrypt + auth
224     else if (no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
225       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_AUTH; // auth-only mode
226     else
227       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE; // no crypto
228 
229     log_debug("WebSocket crypto mode computed: 0x%02x (encrypt=%d, auth=%d)", crypto_mode,
230               ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(crypto_mode), ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(crypto_mode));
231 
232     // Set crypto mode before initialization via callback
233     // NOTE: Always initialize crypto context, even in NONE mode!
234     // The server always sends KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packets, and the context
235     // must be initialized to handle them (even if in disabled/none mode).
236     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
237 
238     log_debug("Initializing crypto context for WebSocket with mode 0x%02x...", crypto_mode);
239 
240     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT8(client_crypto_set_mode, crypto_mode);
241 
242     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
243       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
244       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
245     }
246     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
247 
248     // Get crypto context if ready
249     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
250       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
251     }
252 
253     // Create WebSocket client instance
254     websocket_client_t *ws_client = websocket_client_create("discovery_connection");
255     if (!ws_client) {
256       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket client");
257       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
258       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket client creation failed");
259     }
260 
261     // Connect via WebSocket
262     acip_transport_t *transport = websocket_client_connect(ws_client, ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
263     if (!transport) {
264       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
265       websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
266       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
267       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket connection failed");
268     }
269 
270     // CRITICAL: Wait for the WebSocket handshake to actually complete
271     // The transport is created but the TLS/crypto handshake happens in the service thread
272     log_info("WebSocket transport created, waiting for TLS handshake completion...");
273     const int MAX_HANDSHAKE_WAIT_ITERATIONS = 300; // 300 * 50ms = 15 seconds
274     int handshake_wait_count = 0;
275     while (!acip_transport_is_connected(transport) && handshake_wait_count < MAX_HANDSHAKE_WAIT_ITERATIONS) {
276       platform_sleep_us(50000); // Sleep 50ms between checks
277       handshake_wait_count++;
278       if (handshake_wait_count % 20 == 0) {
279         log_info("WebSocket handshake waiting... (%d seconds elapsed)", handshake_wait_count / 20);
280       }
281     }
282 
283     if (!acip_transport_is_connected(transport)) {
284       log_error("WebSocket TLS handshake failed or timed out after 15 seconds");
285       websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
286       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
287       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket handshake timeout");
288     }
289 
290     log_info("WebSocket connection established to %s", ws_url);
291     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
292     ctx->connection.transport = transport;
293     ctx->connection.transport_type = ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET;
294     ctx->connection.owner = ws_client;
295     ctx->connection.owner_destroy = destroy_websocket_client_owner;
296 
297     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
298   }
299 
300   // TCP connection path (original logic)
301   log_info("Attempting TCP connection to %s:%u (3s timeout)", endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
302 
303   // Transition to attempting state
304   asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
305   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
306     return result;
307   }
308 
309   // Create TCP client internally so connection ownership stays in this layer.
310   tcp_client_t *tcp_client = tcp_client_create();
311   if (!tcp_client) {
312     log_error("Failed to create TCP client");
313     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
314     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "TCP client creation failed");
315   }
316 
317   // Set timeout for this attempt
318   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
319   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
320 
321   // Attempt TCP connection (reconnect_attempt is 0-based, convert for tcp_client_connect)
322   // Use effective_address/port which may be parsed from tcp:// URL scheme
323   int tcp_result = tcp_client_connect(tcp_client, endpoint.host, endpoint.port, (int)ctx->reconnect_attempt,
324                                       ctx->reconnect_attempt == 0, ctx->reconnect_attempt > 0);
325 
326   if (tcp_result != 0) {
327     log_debug("TCP connection failed (tcp_client_connect returned %d)", tcp_result);
328     tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
329     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
330     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "TCP connection failed after %u attempts", ctx->reconnect_attempt);
331   }
332 
333   // Extract socket from TCP client for crypto handshake
334   socket_t sockfd = tcp_client_get_socket(tcp_client);
335   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
336     log_error("Failed to get socket from TCP client");
337     tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
338     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid socket after TCP connection");
339   }
340 
341   // Note: TCP client stores server IP in tcp_client->server_ip for potential crypto context use
342   if (tcp_client->server_ip[0] != '\0') {
343     log_debug("Server IP available from TCP client: %s", tcp_client->server_ip);
344     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(server_connection_set_ip, tcp_client->server_ip);
345   } else {
346     log_warn("TCP client did not populate server_ip field");
347   }
348 
349   // Compute crypto mode from CLI options
350   // Determine if client has authentication material (keys or password)
351   bool has_auth_material =
352       (GET_OPTION(encrypt_key)[0] != '\0' || GET_OPTION(num_identity_keys) > 0 || GET_OPTION(password)[0] != '\0');
353   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
354   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
355 
356   uint8_t crypto_mode;
357   if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && !has_auth_material)
358     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_ENCRYPT; // default: encrypt only
359   else if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
360     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_FULL; // full: encrypt + auth
361   else if (no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
362     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_AUTH; // auth-only mode
363   else
364     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE; // no crypto
365 
366   log_debug("TCP crypto mode computed: 0x%02x (encrypt=%d, auth=%d)", crypto_mode, ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(crypto_mode),
367             ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(crypto_mode));
368 
369   // Setup crypto operations via callbacks
370   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
371   acip_transport_t *transport = NULL;
372 
373   if (crypto_mode != ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE) {
374     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT8(client_crypto_set_mode, crypto_mode);
375 
376     // Initialize crypto context if mode requires handshake (not ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE)
377     // This must happen AFTER setting server IP, as crypto init reads server IP/port
378     log_debug("Initializing crypto context...");
379     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
380       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
381       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
382       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
383     }
384     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
385 
386     // Create transport before handshake (starts with NULL crypto, set after)
387     transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("connection", sockfd, NULL);
388     if (!transport) {
389       log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for TCP");
390       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
391       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create ACIP transport");
392     }
393 
394     // Perform crypto handshake with server
395     log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with server...");
396     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT_TRANSPORT(client_crypto_handshake, transport) != 0) {
397       log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
398       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
399       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
400       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Crypto handshake failed");
401     }
402     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully");
403 
404     // Get crypto context after handshake and set it on the transport
405     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
406       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
407       transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx;
408     }
409   } else {
410     // No crypto — create transport without encryption
411     transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("connection", sockfd, NULL);
412     if (!transport) {
413       log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for TCP");
414       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
415       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create ACIP transport");
416     }
417   }
418 
419   log_info("TCP connection established to %s:%u", endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
420   connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
421   ctx->connection.transport = transport;
422   ctx->connection.transport_type = ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP;
423   ctx->connection.owner = tcp_client;
424   ctx->connection.owner_destroy = destroy_tcp_client_owner;
425   log_debug("TCP owner stored in connection context for cleanup");
426 
427   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
428 }

    ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT, ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE,

    connection_endpoint_protocol_name(),
    connection_endpoint_resolve(), CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET,

    crypto_client_is_ready(), acip_transport::crypto_ctx,

    connection_endpoint_t::input, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,

    connection_endpoint_t::port, connection_endpoint_t::protocol,
    connection_attempt_context_t::reconnect_attempt,

    tcp_client::server_ip, SET_ERRNO, should_exit(),
    tcp_client_connect(), tcp_client_create(),
    tcp_client_destroy(), tcp_client_get_socket(),
    time_get_realtime_ns(),
    connection_attempt_context_t::timeout_ns,
    connection_handle_t::transport,
    connection_handle_t::transport_type,
    websocket_client_connect(), websocket_client_create(), and
    websocket_client_destroy().






asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_websocket
  (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx, const char * ws_url)


Attempt WebSocket connection (ws:// or wss://). Attempt WebSocket
    connection.




Connects to server via WebSocket, performs crypto handshake if
    enabled, and creates ACIP transport for protocol communication.


Definition at line 436 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


436                                                                                                       {
437   log_info("=== connection_attempt_websocket CALLED: url='%s' ===", ws_url);
438 
439   if (!ctx || !ws_url) {
440     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
441   }
442 
443   // Check if shutdown was requested (via callback if available)
444   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
445     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection attempt aborted due to shutdown request");
446   }
447 
448   log_info("Attempting WebSocket connection to %s", ws_url);
449 
450   // Transition to attempting state
451   asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
452   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
453     return result;
454   }
455 
456   // Set timeout for this attempt
457   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
458   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
459 
460   // Initialize crypto context if encryption is enabled
461   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
462   if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
463     log_debug("Initializing crypto context for WebSocket...");
464 
465     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
466       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
467       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
468     }
469     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
470 
471     // Get crypto context if ready
472     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
473       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
474     }
475   }
476 
477   // Create WebSocket client instance
478   websocket_client_t *ws_client = websocket_client_create("connection");
479   if (!ws_client) {
480     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket client");
481     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
482     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket client creation failed");
483   }
484 
485   // Connect via WebSocket
486   acip_transport_t *transport = websocket_client_connect(ws_client, ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
487   if (!transport) {
488     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
489     websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
490     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
491     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket connection failed");
492   }
493 
494   log_info("WebSocket connection established to %s", ws_url);
495   connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
496   ctx->connection.transport = transport;
497   ctx->connection.transport_type = ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET;
498   ctx->connection.owner = ws_client;
499   ctx->connection.owner_destroy = destroy_websocket_client_owner;
500   log_debug("WebSocket owner stored in connection context");
501 
502   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
503 }

bool connection_check_timeout (const
  connection_attempt_context_t * ctx)


Check if connection attempt has exceeded timeout. Compares elapsed
    time since attempt start against timeout.




Parameters


ctx Connection context


Returns


true if timeout exceeded, false otherwise


Definition at line 141 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


141                                                                        {
142   if (!ctx)
143     return false;
144 
145   uint64_t elapsed_ns = time_get_realtime_ns() - ctx->attempt_start_time_ns;
146   bool timeout_exceeded = elapsed_ns > ctx->timeout_ns;
147 
148   if (timeout_exceeded) {
149     char elapsed_str[32], timeout_str[32];
150     time_pretty(elapsed_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
151     time_pretty(ctx->timeout_ns, -1, timeout_str, sizeof(timeout_str));
152     log_warn("Connection timeout exceeded: elapsed %s > %s limit", elapsed_str, timeout_str);
153   }
154 
155   return timeout_exceeded;
156 }






References
    connection_attempt_context_t::attempt_start_time_ns, log_warn,
    time_get_realtime_ns(), time_pretty(), and
    connection_attempt_context_t::timeout_ns.






void
  connection_context_cleanup (connection_attempt_context_t *
  ctx)


Cleanup connection attempt context. Closes and releases any active
    transports. Called on connection success, failure, or shutdown.




Parameters


ctx Connection context to cleanup


Definition at line 111 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


111                                                                    {
112   if (!ctx)
113     return;
114 
115   connection_handle_cleanup(&ctx->connection);
116 
117   log_debug("Connection context cleaned up");
118 }

asciichat_error_t connection_context_init
  (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx)


Initialize connection attempt context. Sets up initial state,
    resets timeouts, and prepares for connection attempt.




Parameters


ctx Connection context to initialize


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 82 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


82                                                                              {
83   if (!ctx) {
84     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Context pointer is NULL");
85   }
86 
87   // Reset context
88   memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(connection_attempt_context_t));
89   connection_handle_init(&ctx->connection);
90 
91   // Initialize state
92   ctx->current_state = CONN_STATE_IDLE;
93   ctx->previous_state = CONN_STATE_IDLE;
94 
95   // Initialize timeout
96   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
97   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
98 
99   // Initialize counters
100   ctx->reconnect_attempt = 1;
101   ctx->total_transitions = 0;
102 
103   log_debug("Connection context initialized");
104 
105   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
106 }

const
  char * connection_state_name (connection_state_t state)


Get human-readable state name for logging.


Parameters


state Connection state


Returns


Static string representation (e.g.,
  "CONNECTED")


Definition at line 58 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


58                                                             {
59   switch (state) {
60   case CONN_STATE_IDLE:
61     return "IDLE";
62   case CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING:
63     return "ATTEMPTING";
64   case CONN_STATE_CONNECTED:
65     return "CONNECTED";
66   case CONN_STATE_DISCONNECTED:
67     return "DISCONNECTED";
68   case CONN_STATE_FAILED:
69     return "FAILED";
70   default:
71     return "UNKNOWN";
72   }
73 }

asciichat_error_t connection_state_transition
  (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx, connection_state_t
  new_state)


Transition to next connection state with validation. Transition to
    next connection state.


Definition at line 123 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


123                                                                                                                {
124   if (!ctx) {
125     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Context pointer is NULL");
126   }
127 
128   // Store previous state
129   ctx->previous_state = ctx->current_state;
130   ctx->current_state = new_state;
131   ctx->total_transitions++;
132 
133   log_debug("State transition: %s → %s", connection_state_name(ctx->previous_state), connection_state_name(new_state));
134 
135   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
136 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, connection_state_name(),
    connection_attempt_context_t::current_state,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    connection_attempt_context_t::previous_state, SET_ERRNO, and
    connection_attempt_context_t::total_transitions.


Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp(), and
    connection_attempt_websocket().

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
